title: Architecture
content:
Architecture
Compendus is a full-stack application with a web frontend, server API,
and native iOS app. The web and iOS clients communicate with the server
through a shared REST API.
System Overview
Web Application
The web app uses React Server Components via react-flight-router with
Vite as the build tool and Hono as the server framework.
Key Technologies
- React 19 + RSC — Server Components for data fetching, Client Components for interactivity
- react-flight-router — File-based routing with React Server Components support
- Vite 8 — Dev server with HMR, production bundler
- Hono — Lightweight HTTP server framework for API routes
- Drizzle ORM — Type-safe SQL query builder for SQLite
- Tailwind CSS 4 — Utility-first CSS with design token system
Project Structure
Data Flow
1. Server components in app/routes/ fetch data directly from the database
2. Data is passed to client components via props (RSC serialization)
3. Client components use useSearchParams() for URL state and server actions for mutations
4. API routes in server/ handle REST requests from the iOS app and external clients
iOS Application
The iOS app is a native SwiftUI application with SwiftData for local
persistence. It communicates with the server via REST API for library
syncing and book downloads.
Key Technologies
- SwiftUI — Declarative UI framework for all views
- SwiftData — Local persistence for downloaded books and settings
- Custom Reader Engine — Native EPUB (CoreText + UITextView) and PDF (PDFKit) rendering
- ZIPFoundation — EPUB extraction from ZIP archives
- Whisper.cpp — On-device audiobook transcription
- TTS Engine — Text-to-speech with pre-generation and caching
Reader Engine
Database Schema
Compendus uses SQLite with Drizzle ORM. The schema is defined in app/lib/db/schema.ts.
 Description |
|
 Core book collection with metadata, file info, and reading state |
 User-created groupings with hierarchical support |
 Junction table linking books to collections |

title: Database Schema
content:
Compendus uses SQLite with Drizzle ORM. The schema is defined in app/lib/db/schema.ts.
 Description |
|
 Core book collection with metadata, file info, and reading state |
 User-created groupings with hierarchical support |
 Junction table linking books to collections |
 Color-coded tags for categorization |
 Junction table linking books to tags |
 Position-based bookmarks within books |
 Text highlights with notes and colors |
 Reading time tracking (start/end, pages read) |
 Wishlist items with metadata from external sources |

title: Background Processing
content:
Long-running operations are handled by a background job queue with
real-time progress tracking via Server-Sent Events (SSE).
- Format conversion — PDF/MOBI/AZW3 to EPUB
- Audiobook transcription — Whisper-based speech to text
- TTS pre-generation — Text-to-speech audio caching
- Multi-file audio merge — Combining audio tracks into single audiobook
Job progress can be polled via GET /api/jobs/:id or streamed via GET /api/jobs/:id/progress (SSE).

title: Getting Started
content:
Getting Started
Set up Compendus on your local machine or deploy with Docker.
Prerequisites
- Node.js 20+ — Required runtime
- pnpm — Package manager (npm install -g pnpm)
- Git LFS — Required for cloning (large binary tracking)
Installation
Clone the repository and install dependencies:
Database Setup
Compendus uses SQLite with Drizzle ORM. Initialize the database:
The database file is stored at data/compendus.db. Book files are stored in data/books/ and covers in data/covers/.
Environment Variables
Create a .env file in the project root:
The Google Books API key is optional but recommended for automatic metadata fetching. Without it, metadata must be entered manually.
Development Server
Start the development server:
The app will be available at http://localhost:3000. The dev server includes hot module reloading, API routes, and React Server Components support.
Production Build
The build process compiles worker threads, bundles React Server Components, and copies database migrations.
Docker Deployment
Compendus includes Docker support for easy deployment:
Mount the data/ directory as a volume to persist your library across container restarts.

|
 Start development server with HMR |
 Build for production |
 Start production server |
 Generate database schema |
 Run database migrations |
 Open Drizzle Studio (DB browser) |
 Run test suite |
 Run tests in watch mode |
 Run linter (oxfmt + oxlint) |

title: Compendus Documentation
content:
Compendus Documentation
Compendus is a self-hosted personal digital library manager. Upload,
organize, and read books across web and iOS with automatic metadata,
full-text search, and reading progress tracking.
Quick Links
- Getting Started — Install and run Compendus locally
- API Reference — Browse all REST API endpoints
- Architecture — Understand the technical design
- Supported Formats — View all supported file types
- iOS App — Set up the native iOS client
Features
- Multi-Format Support — Read and manage PDF, EPUB, MOBI, CBR, CBZ ebooks, M4B/MP3/M4A audiobooks, and comic archives.
- Automatic Metadata — Fetch book metadata, covers, and descriptions from Google Books and Open Library automatically.
- Reading Progress — Track reading progress, create highlights with notes, bookmark pages, and log reading sessions.
- iOS & Web — Native iOS app with custom EPUB/PDF reader engine plus a full-featured responsive web interface.
- Full-Text Search — Search across titles, authors, descriptions, and optionally within book content itself.
- Collections & Tags — Organize your library with hierarchical collections, color-coded tags, and series tracking.
Tech Stack
React 19, Vite, Hono, SQLite, Tailwind CSS, Drizzle ORM, SwiftUI, SwiftData

content:
- Getting Started — Install and run Compendus locally
- API Reference — Browse all REST API endpoints

content:
- Multi-Format Support — Read and manage PDF, EPUB, MOBI, CBR, CBZ ebooks, M4B/MP3/M4A audiobooks, and comic archives.
- Automatic Metadata — Fetch book metadata, covers, and descriptions from Google Books and Open Library automatically.
- Reading Progress — Track reading progress, create highlights with notes, bookmark pages, and log reading sessions.
- iOS & Web — Native iOS app with custom EPUB/PDF reader engine plus a full-featured responsive web interface.
- Full-Text Search — Search across titles, authors, descriptions, and optionally within book content itself.
- Collections & Tags — Organize your library with hierarchical collections, color-coded tags, and series tracking.

title: iOS App
content:
iOS App
Compendus includes a native iOS app built with SwiftUI and SwiftData
that connects to the server for library management and book downloads.
Prerequisites
- Xcode 15+ — Required for building the iOS app
- iOS 17+ — Minimum deployment target
- Compendus server — Running locally or on the network for the iOS app to connect to
Setup
1. Open the Xcode project at Compendus/Compendus.xcodeproj
2. Swift Package Manager dependencies (ZIPFoundation, SwiftSoup) will resolve automatically
3. Configure the server URL in the app's settings screen to point to your Compendus server
4. Build and run on a device or simulator
The dev server is configured with host: true so the iOS simulator can connect via the local network.
Features
- Library Browsing — Browse your full library with search, filtering by format/type, and sorting options.
- Offline Reading — Download books for offline reading with background download support and progress tracking.
- Custom EPUB Reader — Native CoreText + UITextView-based EPUB renderer with pagination, highlights, and bookmarks.
- PDF Reader — PDFKit-based PDF viewer with annotation support and highlight persistence.
- Audiobook Player — Audio playback with chapter navigation, speed control, and sleep timer.
- Highlights & Notes — Create color-coded text highlights with notes. Synced to the server via REST API.
- Reading Sessions — Automatic reading time tracking with pages-read statistics.
- On-Device Transcription — Transcribe audiobooks locally using Whisper.cpp without sending audio to external services.
- Text-to-Speech — Built-in TTS with pre-generation and audio caching for read-along functionality.
- Home Screen Widget — WidgetKit widget showing currently reading books on the iOS home screen.
- Deep Linking — Open books directly via compendus://book/{bookId} URL scheme.
- Dark Mode — Full dark mode support with customizable reading themes (font, size, line height).
Project Structure
Reader Engine
The iOS app uses a custom-built reader en

title: Reader Engine
content: The iOS app uses a custom-built reader engine with native iOS rendering.

title: EPUB Rendering Pipeline
content:
1. EPUBParser — Extracts ZIP, parses OPF metadata and spine order
2. XHTMLContentParser — Parses XHTML into ContentNode AST (headings, paragraphs, images, lists)
3. AttributedStringBuilder — Converts ContentNode AST into NSAttributedString with styles
4. NativePaginationEngine — Uses CoreText CTFramesetter to paginate text
5. NativePageViewController — Hosts UITextView for each page with gesture handling

title: Reader Features
content:
- Text highlights with XPath-based range serialization
- epub:type footnote detection with popover presentation
- Fixed-layout (FXL) rendering with rendition:spread blank page detection
- SVG cover detection and rendering
- Background chapter parsing with async image pre-loading
- Session-scoped NSCache-based image caching

title: Server Communication
content:
The iOS app communicates with the Compendus server through the REST API. Key interactions:
 Endpoint |
|
 GET /api/library |
 GET /api/search?q=... |
 GET /api/books/:id |
 GET /books/:id.:format |
 GET /covers/:id.jpg |
 PUT /api/books/:id |
 PUT /api/books/:id/transcript |
 GET /api/reader/:id/resource/ |

title: API Reference
content: REST API reference for Compendus. Browse endpoints for books, search, upload, reader, convert, transcribe, collections, tags, bookmarks, highlights, reading sessions, wishlist. Base URL is /api. No authentication required for local deployments. CORS enabled.

title: Supported Formats
content: EPUB PDF MOBI AZW3 CBZ CBR M4B M4A MP3 ebook comic audiobook formats. EPUB is the standard ebook format with full reading support. PDF rendered as page images. MOBI and AZW3 auto-convert to EPUB. CBZ comic ZIP archive. CBR auto-converts to CBZ. M4B Apple audiobook format. Cover images extracted automatically. Conversion support: PDF to EPUB, MOBI to EPUB, AZW3 to EPUB, CBR to CBZ.
